lede: A look at September 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including John Dalton and Franz Josef Strauss, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Supreme Court of India
events.desc: Supreme Court of India decriminalised all consensual sex among adults in private, making homosexuality legal on the Indian lands.

events.title: Funeral of Diana, Princess of Wales
events.desc: The funeral of Diana, Princess of Wales takes place in London. Well over a million people line the streets and 21⁄2 billion watch around the world on television.

events.title: Cal Ripken Jr.
events.desc: Cal Ripken Jr. of the Baltimore Orioles plays in his 2,131st consecutive game, breaking a record that had stood for 56 years.

events.title: 1952 Farnborough Airshow crash
events.desc: A prototype aircraft crashes at the Farnborough Airshow in Hampshire, England, killing 29 spectators and the two on board.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The British Royal Air Force suffers its first fighter pilot casualty of the Second World War at the Battle of Barking Creek as a result of friendly fire.

events.title: Hipólito Yrigoyen
events.desc: Democratically elected Argentine president Hipólito Yrigoyen is deposed in a military coup.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The First Battle of the Marne, which would halt the Imperial German Army's advance into France, begins.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Battle of Groton Heights takes place, resulting in a British victory.

events.title: Thirty Years' War
events.desc: Thirty Years' War: In the Battle of Nördlingen, the Catholic Imperial army defeats Swedish and German Protestant forces.

events.title: Christopher Columbus
events.desc: Christopher Columbus sails from La Gomera in the Canary Islands, his final port of call before crossing the Atlantic Ocean for the first time.

births.name: Franz Josef Strauss
births.line: German lieutenant and politician, Minister President of Bavaria

births.name: Joseph Wirth
births.line: German educator and politician, Chancellor of Germany

births.name: Chris Christie
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 55th Governor of New Jersey

deaths.text: Robert Mugabe, Zimbabwean politician, 2nd President of Zimbabwe
Michael S. Hart, American author, founded Project Gutenberg
Akira Kurosawa, Japanese director, producer, and screenwriter

observances.text: Christian feast days: Faustus, Abibus and Dionysius of Alexandria
